focus_points.label: How she’s landing with the team right now.
focus_points.reason: Since the reorg, she has pulled back in leadership forums and appears less connected in team interactions. One possibility is a temporary transition load; another is a mismatch in scope or support — worth clarifying what’s changed in her day-to-day connection and where she feels least “in it.”

focus_points.label: Leadership forums vs delivery focus shift.
focus_points.reason: Since the reorg, she’s still delivering but has pulled back in leadership forums. Could be a short-term reprioritization, or it could signal she’s not getting the right conditions to lead in those spaces — worth clarifying what she’s saying yes and no to right now.

focus_points.label: What she’d want more of from you.
focus_points.reason: Whether she’s asking for help or not, the manager-support conversation matters in a “something feels off” 1:1 because senior people often manage strain quietly; you want to learn what support would actually reduce friction in the next two weeks, not just acknowledge it.

focus_points.label: Workload and capacity after the reorg.
focus_points.reason: Whether the pullback is temporary transition load or something deeper, workload and capacity is the fastest way to separate “busy but okay” from “busy and withdrawing.” At senior EM level, it’s also where you can remove constraints quickly if the calendar or interrupt load is the driver.
